title: Auto-execute medium-risk follow-ups with revert commands in digest
description: Medium-risk follow-up actions currently require CEO batch-approval via AskUserQuestion. This slows directive completion without meaningful safety gain. Change to: auto-execute medium-risk, include git revert commands in the digest so CEO can undo if needed. CEO decision: remove immediately, no probation period.

in: Change medium-risk follow-up handling from batch-approval to auto-execute + report
Generate git revert commands for each auto-executed medium-risk action
Add Revert Commands section to digest template
Update risk classification docs to reflect new authority levels

dod.criterion: Medium-risk follow-ups auto-execute without CEO approval gate
Digest includes revert commands for every auto-executed medium-risk action
Revert commands are copy-pasteable and actually work

tasks.title: Update follow-up handling in pipeline step docs: medium-risk -> auto-execute
tasks.scope: Modify wrapup pipeline doc: remove AskUserQuestion gate for medium-risk, change to auto-execute + log pattern matching low-risk

tasks.title: Add revert command generation to follow-up execution logic
tasks.scope: After each medium-risk auto-execute, capture the git commit hash and generate copy-pasteable revert command

tasks.title: Update digest template with Revert Commands section
tasks.scope: Add '## Revert Commands' section to digest reference template listing each medium-risk action + its revert command

tasks.title: Smoke test: run directive with medium-risk follow-up, verify auto-execute + revert
tasks.scope: End-to-end test with a real directive that produces medium-risk follow-ups
